title: Cannot pause rootless container (cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"), container cgroup is empty
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Using rootless podman, cannot pause a container. All containers have an empty cgroup, which seems to be the root cause of the problem.

System is using cgroups v2 (without systemd) but they do not really seem to be used at all.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

This is a stock Debian Bullseye with some configuration hardening.
Kernel has command-line options `cgroup_enable=memory swapaccount=1` and sysctl option `kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=0`
`/proc` is mounted with options `hidepid=2,gid=procadmin` and non-root user is **not** in `procadmin` group

1. `podman run --name=sleeper -d --rm debian:buster sleep inf`
2. `podman pause sleeper`
3. see error

**Describe the results you received:**
```
2022-01-09T18:45:49.000950560Z: cannot pause the container without a cgroup
Error: `/usr/bin/crun pause b4e7e2a6613a46fafb11ffd17576e67081ec6e1d1f63232f35cd1170f2a32ed0` failed: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error and `podman pause` should successfully pause the container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The machine is using cgroups v2 but when I inspect the running container I see that there is no cgroup path so this container never got one; I am using `cgroupfs` as manager and not `systemd`.

The user is logged in via SSH (root) and then switched to non-root with `su -l non-root-user`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


I am using the latest version released (`deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/Debian_11/ /`) and checked that changelog between 3.4.2 and 3.4.4 does not have relevant changes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical

comments.body: cgroups are not used by rootless when running with cgroupfs.   You need to setup one manually and then use `--cgroup-parent` if you really want to use them

@giuseppe thanks, I start to understand things better. Shall we update documentation about this? I could not find anything (my bad if documentation is already clear and I am failing to find/interpret it).

Current docs say (https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman.1.html?highlight=cgroupfs#cgroup-manager-manager):
> Note: CGroup manager is not supported in rootless mode when using CGroups Version V1.

Instead it could say:
> Note: CGroup manager is not supported in rootless mode when using CGroups Version V1; when using 'cgroupfs' in rootless mode you must use --cgroup-parent with a manually-setup cgroup.

The in-comments documentation in containers.conf could also mention something about this.
@gdm85 are you interested in opening a PR to improve the documentation?

Sure, why not. I am just new around here: would changing some file under `docs/` in this repository be sufficient? (and perhaps the default `containers.conf`, if I manage to find it).

The Wiki does not seem to contain much about this so I guess I do not need to improve it.

> Sure, why not. I am just new around here: would changing some file under `docs/` in this repository be sufficient? (and perhaps the default `containers.conf`, if I manage to find it).

yes thanks, that would be enough.
